what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard item that records a medium-low concern about adopting Obsidian: the `.obsidian/` directory becomes state the build must version and sync, creates per-device divergence (especially mobile plugin limitations), and adds another sync-conflict surface.
observations: YAML front matter includes id, board:// address, type=block, source=claude_code, state=current, channel=dragnet-development, and a part_of link to item-48cd6801
Created and updated timestamps are identical
Title is labeled P12 · 3.2 and marked (MEDIUM-LOW
Body lists three impacts: state to version, per-device divergence, and sync-conflict surface
It references §2.1/§2.2 as showing the benefit is thin
standouts: Mobile may not run the community plugins that provide the readable-title feature, undermining the one place it is needed
The `.obsidian/` directory is framed as build-managed, versioned state
It explicitly questions the value of the benefit given prior sections
conventions: Uses YAML front matter with ledger-style metadata (id, address, type, source, state, links, timestamps)
Uses board:// URI scheme for item identity and part_of relationships
Markdown body uses a numbered section heading and bullet list
Priority is embedded in the title rather than a separate field
concerns: The title appears to have an unclosed parenthesis: '(MEDIUM-LOW'
Created and updated timestamps are identical, so there is no edit history beyond initial filing
It cites §2.1/§2.2 without including or linking to them, so the claim is not locally verifiable
notes: A dragnet-development noticeboard item flagging that Obsidian's `.obsidian/` config is a hidden, ongoing ownership and sync cost, with a specific mobile/desktop divergence risk.
questions: What do P12 and the section numbering signify?
What specifically do §2.1 and §2.2 say about the thin benefit?
What is the 'readable-title trick' and which plugin provides it?
What is the parent item-48cd6801 and how does this item relate to it?
What is the scope of the dragnet-development channel?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to record a medium-low concern about Obsidian config ownership; the content matches that purpose.
apparent_themes: Configuration as a managed build artifact
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to surface a hidden operational cost of adopting Obsidian by treating the `.obsidian/` directory as a first-class, versioned, sync-sensitive asset and warning that mobile plugin gaps may defeat a key usability feature.
novelty: It explicitly reclassifies Obsidian's hidden plugin/settings directory as a build-managed state artifact and ties desktop/mobile plugin divergence to the failure of a readability affordance.
contribution: It captures a concrete, device-specific risk (mobile plugin unavailability breaking a readable-title feature) and a reproducibility requirement (versioning `.obsidian/`) that any fused evaluation of Obsidian adoption would need to account for.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard item in dragnet-development argues that adopting Obsidian adds a `.obsidian/` configuration directory that must be versioned and synced, with per-device plugin divergence and sync-conflict overhead, and questions the benefit given prior sections.
intention_for_embedding: To record a medium-low risk that Obsidian's hidden config directory becomes an ongoing ownership and synchronization burden, especially across desktop and mobile.
